_schema.description: Family tree people. Each fact has a confidence level and source list.

confidence_levels.confirmed: Verified by primary document (birth cert, archive record, etc.)
confidence_levels.documented: Stated in a document; document believed correct but needs cross-check
confidence_levels.family_oral: Told by family member or written in Leah's memoir
confidence_levels.hypothesis: Researcher's working theory, not yet supported

_schema.sources: References by id: doc_xxx for documents.json, msg_NNN for messages.json, oral_NN for family oral history, report_XXX for genealogist's reports

people.note_en: Dov Rapaport — Dalia's husband, father of Doron, Dana and Daniel. The man this archive is for — an 80th birthday gift from his family, August 2026.

Paternal grandfather of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Father of Dov. Holocaust survivor who escaped Nazi-occupied Galicia, reached Brussels in April 1946, and eventually settled in Israel. Forestry engineer, polyglot, member of Betar (revisionist Zionist youth movement).

birth.note_en: BREAKTHROUGH 15 May 2026: birth certificate found in Nadwórna by Ms. Kasia at the Jewish Historical Institute. The 1946 Brussels DP card lists Cieszyn/Tesin but that was likely a French clerk's mishearing — Nadwórna is the verified birthplace.

death.note_en: Deceased. Specific date to be added by family.

facts.value: Forestry engineer
Polish (native); Italian, German (fluent); Czech, Russian
Member of Betar (Poland)
facts.note: Doron stated 17 May 2026 in WhatsApp: 'David as a Betar member wanted to go to Israel'. Jabotinsky Institute online archive returned no match (see h_betar_records); physical-file check still pending.

facts.value: Hid in a niche between cut wooden logs in a railway wagon to escape the ghetto liquidation
facts.note: Memoir page 41: 'when they began the liquidation of the Jews I hid between these wooden planks' — confirming the family's escape story.

facts.value: Wanted to go to Israel (as a Betar member, vs Leah whose affidavit was for USA)
Lwów, Poland (1 Jan 1938)
Stripped of Polish citizenship
Registered as Displaced Person in Brussels (Belgium), 9 April 1946
Wood expert at Israeli customs authority
Escaped Nadwórna ghetto in a railway wagon hidden in a niche carved between wooden logs, reached Lwów
Worked on railway/road construction in Lwów under fake identity

people.note_en: Paternal grandmother of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Mother of Dov. Holocaust survivor who escaped the Nazi persecution of Galician Jewry by living under multiple false identities (including 'Maria Cizlik'), managing an antiques store in Lwów on the Aryan side. Wrote a memoir of the war.

birth.note_en: Bolechów birth record found for 'Lea' born 8.4.1913 to Elias and Matel Weitzner. Family death certificate lists 6.4.1916. The Polish Jewish Historical Institute index also lists Lusia Rappoport born 1916-04-08. There may be a 1913 vs 1916 conflict between civil record and family record. Lea declared age 18 in 1935 → would imply 1917 birth. Per Basia: brides often understated their age.

facts.value: Married David Rapaport, February 1938, in Muszyna (מושינה)
facts.note: Memoir page 17 explicitly: marriage took place 'in the small resort town' (Muszyna), where they settled. Memoir page 16 dates the marriage as 'בפברואר 1938' (February 1938).

facts.value: She was 18, he was 24 at marriage (per memoir)
Lived in Lwów under fake identity 'Maria Cizlik' (borrowed)
Legionów Street 24, Lwów (with Hormek fake documents) — opposite the theater
facts.note: Memoir page 40 confirms: 'her apartment on Legionów Street 24, opposite the theater'.

facts.value: Manager of art-effects/antiques store under fake identity, ~1941
Briefly worked as teller / in the passport section in Przemyśl, 1939, during the Soviet occupation
facts.note: The historical context of this period is the Soviet 'passportization' campaign that all residents of border towns had to navigate — see IPN paper. Her stay was short; she spent most of the war elsewhere, escaping Nazi persecution.

facts.value: Received an affidavit to emigrate to the USA (per Doron 17 May)
Appears in Polish post-war records 1945-46; not yet located in Brussels 1946 DP records under any spelling. Likely traveled separately from David before they reunited in Brussels for Dov's birth.
Managed a hotel in Muszyna (the resort town) where 'all the social cream' of the town's visitors stayed
facts.note: Memoir page 18: 'all the social elite of Muszyna came to the hotel which Lusia managed energetically'.

facts.value: Lived with relatives in Lwów, attended Carpathian forestry school filings
Wrote memoir of the war (Hebrew)

people.note_en: Older brother of Dov. Uncle to Doron, Dana and Daniel. Survived the war as a child. Married Tamar; father of Ronen (older) and Hadas.

birth.note_en: Found in CKŻP (Central Committee of Polish Jews) post-war registration records. Birthplace per family memoir was 'Mosina' — but the location of 'Mosina' is itself a research mystery (see hypotheses).

facts.value: Memoir mentions taking baby Shimon to a river in 'Mosina'
Registered in CKŻP records as son of Lusia and Dawid Rapaport
Married Tamar; two children Ronen (older son) and Hadas (younger sister)

people.note_en: Shimon's wife. Aunt to Doron, Dana and Daniel. Memoir page 83 names her as 'Tami' — the everyday short form of Tamar.

Polish Catholic peasant woman from the Nadwórna area whose birth certificate Lusia used to forge her wartime identity papers (the 'Maria' name). Per memoir p. 51, she protected Shimon in Nadwórna 1942-1945. Lusia called her identity 'Maria Ciccelik' throughout the war — earlier family oral records had this as 'Maria Cizlik' (a memory simplification).

facts.value: Lent her birth certificate to Lusia for the wartime Aryan-side false identity
Sheltered baby/toddler Shimon in Nadwórna 1942-1945 (per memoir p. 51)

people.note_en: Gentile neighbor in Nadwórna who hid David and Lusia in the forest while caring for baby Shimon (memoir p. 26). Risked his life to shelter Jewish friends during the German occupation.

facts.value: Hid David and Lusia in the forest near Nadwórna; cared for Shimon while parents hid

people.note_en: Russian Gentile neighbor in Nadwórna, father of three. Per memoir p. 30: arranged the secret transfer of Shimon from Nadwórna to Lwów and warned Lusia of a planned Nazi action against Jewish children.

facts.value: Arranged Shimon's transfer from Nadwórna to Lwów; warned of a planned action against children

people.note_en: Gentile family (Nadwórna + Lwów) who helped Lusia with food, housing, and the escape route to Lwów. The sister Mrs. Inovska sheltered Shimon in Lwów (memoir p. 30). The family name 'Hormak' is the documented source of the 'Hormek papers' family memory previously had recorded.

facts.value: Provided food and housing in Nadwórna; helped the family escape to Lwów; sheltered Shimon there
Family memory of 'Hormek papers' likely refers to documents arranged by or through the Hormak family — not a document type name

people.note_en: David's sister. Lived in the Lwów ghetto with her family. Per memoir p. 37: bought forged US emigration documents but was reportedly betrayed by the sellers; later disappeared in the war (presumed murdered in the Holocaust). The family memory of her was reconfirmed by Dalia 17 May 2026.

facts.value: Lived in Lwów (ghetto from 1941)
Bought forged US emigration documents to try to escape the ghetto; sellers reportedly informed (per memoir p. 41)
Disappeared during the Holocaust — fate not in memoir but inferred to be murdered
Dalia confirmed Lota's existence 17 May 2026 WhatsApp

people.note_en: Older son of Shimon and Tamar. First cousin to Doron, Dana and Daniel.
Younger sister of Ronen, daughter of Shimon and Tamar. First cousin to Doron, Dana and Daniel.
Paternal great-grandfather of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Merchant in Nadwórna. Father of David.

facts.value: Merchant (kupiec) in Nadwórna
A Bernard Rapaport appears in Przemyśl records around 1910 — may or may not be him
Issachar Berish (יששכר בעריש)
Marriage to Rivka Griffel of Nadwórna documented in print by Edward Gelles, "Griffel of Nadworna" (Balliol College Oxford archives), entry #8 in the Eliezer Griffel pedigree.

death.note_en: Almost certainly perished in the Holocaust. Most likely scenario: shot in the 6 October 1941 Bukowinka Forest mass aktion alongside ~2,000 Nadwórna Jews, including his brother-in-law David Mendel Griffel and sister-in-law Chawa Wahl. Alternative: 24 October 1942 final Nadwórna ghetto liquidation. No Page of Testimony was ever filed for him. Notable: Berisz is absent from the 1932 Nadwórna town taxpayer list and from the 1975 Yizkor necrology, suggesting he may have lived in a village outside Nadwórna town — likely tied to the Griffel timber/oil business locations.

people.note_en: Paternal great-grandmother of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Mother of David. Birth record from Nadwórna found 15 May 2026 by Ms. Kasia at ŻIH Warsaw — the family's breakthrough document.

facts.value: Vacationed in Krynica (resort) in summer 1912 — registered at Alfredówka on 25 July 1912, listed as wife of a Nadwórna merchant. Newspaper record.
Documented as entry #8 of the 10 children of Eliezer (Zeida) Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes in Edward Gelles, "Griffel of Nadworna".

death.note_en: Almost certainly perished in the Holocaust alongside her husband Berisz. Most likely scenario: shot in the 6 October 1941 Bukowinka Forest mass aktion alongside her brother David Mendel Griffel and sister-in-law Chawa Wahl. No Page of Testimony was ever filed for her — Doron should file one.

people.note_en: Great-great-grandfather (paternal-maternal line) of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Head of the Jewish community of Nadwórna (Av Kehillah). Patriarch of a large extended family. Major Galician industrialist: leased oil deposits and forests, ran a substantial timber export business reaching Vienna. Hager-Ottynia Chasid — he and his sons-in-law prayed at the Ottynia rebbe's synagogue. Documented in Edward Gelles, "Griffel of Nadworna" (Balliol College Oxford archives). Had ten children with his wife Sarah Matel Chajes of Kolomea.

facts.value: Head of Jewish community (Av Kehillah) of Nadwórna
Oil deposits, forests, timber export across Galicia and to Vienna
Hager Chasid (Ottynia synagogue)
Father of 10 children, including Rivka (Rebeka) Griffel-Rapaport b.1888
Chajes rabbinical dynasty of Kolomea
Isaac Chaim Chayes (1823-1866) of Kolomea → Isaac ben Abraham Chayes (1538-1617, Chief Rabbi of Prague). Lineage includes David Halevi Segal (Taz, Lvov) and Joel Sirkes (Bach, Cracow).

birth.note_en: Born in Kolomea (Kolomyia), Galicia — exact year unknown but ~1855.

people.note_en: Great-great-grandmother (paternal-maternal line) of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Born in Kolomea (Kolomyia), Galicia. Descendant of the Chajes rabbinical dynasty — her ancestor Isaac ben Abraham Chayes (1538-1617) was Chief Rabbi of Prague. Through this line the family connects to David Halevi Segal (the "Taz", Chief Rabbi of Lvov) and Joel Sirkes (the "Bach", Chief Rabbi of Cracow) — a 350+ year rabbinical pedigree.
Leah's father. Started as cattle dealer in Bolechów Ruski, later tannery owner.

facts.value: Cattle dealer (handlarz bydłem) in Bolechów Ruski, c. 1911-16
Tannery in Bolechów, listed in 1930 directory — confirmed Weitzner spelling
Emigrated to Mandatory Palestine in 1933 with capital permit (1000 GBP capitalist visa, British Mandate)
Married Matel Weinreb in Dolina, 1905 (statutory civil marriage)

people.note_en: Leah's mother. From Dolina (the district seat near Bolechów).

facts.value: From Dolina
Tavern keeper in Dolina

people.note_en: Leah's older sister. Feige (Yiddish) = Tzipora (Hebrew).

facts.value: Emigrated to Israel with family ~1933

people.note_en: Leah's younger brother.

facts.value: Emigrated to Israel with family ~1933

facts.value: Known by Hebrew name only
Emigrated to Israel with family ~1933

Mother of Doron, Dana and Daniel. Wife of Dov. Equal partner in this family research project.

One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna. Eldest son of Eliezer Griffel. Took over most of the family timber/oil business. Father of Zygmunt (1897-1951), Regina (1900-1954, mother of Edward Gelles), Edward (1904-1959). Murdered with wife Chawa in 1941, almost certainly the 6 October 1941 Bukowinka Forest mass shooting of ~2,000 Nadwórna Jews. David "Memek" Rapaport b.1911 was named after him in Ashkenazi naming tradition. Married Chawa Wahl (1877-1941, also perished).
One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna.  Married Benzion Wilner.

One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna. Her son was Dr Arnold Lamm; her grandson is Dr Steven Lamm (b.1948, NYC, NYU Langone — living 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek). Married Zygmunt (Shalom) Lamm.

One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna. Had 10 children. His eldest son Dr Jacob Griffel (1900-1962) was a famous Vaad ha-Hatzala rescuer based in Istanbul during WWII. Another son Yehoshua Heshel "Henry" Griffel emigrated to New Jersey; his son Andrew Griffel is alive — living 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek. Married Judith Breit (d.1938).
One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna.  Married Adela Englard.
One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna.  Married Ziporah.
One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna.  Married Chana Kahan.
One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna.  Married Bonya (Abraham) Ohrenstein.

One of the 10 children of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes of Nadwórna. Her grandson via son Samuel was Yehuda Nir (Juliusz Gruenfeld, 1930-2014 NYC), Holocaust survivor and author of 'The Lost Childhood' memoir — first cousin once removed of David Memek. Married Shlomo Gruenfeld (1869-1953).
Father of Eliezer "Zeida" Griffel (1850-1918) of Nadwórna. Edward Gelles places the Griffel rabbinical-merchant line in the 18th-century Lemberg circle of Rav Leiser Neches and Rav Jacob Leib.
Mother of Eliezer "Zeida" Griffel of Nadwórna. Recorded in Edward Gelles, "Griffel of Nadworna".

facts.value: NYU Langone Preston Robert Tisch Center for Men's Health, NYC

people.note_en: LIVING 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek Rapaport (b.1948). Internist at NYU Langone Health, New York City; medical director of the Preston Robert Tisch Center for Men's Health. Author of 'The Hardness Factor', 'No Guts No Glory'. Regular Today Show medical contributor 2000s. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Zissel Griffel-Lamm → Dr Arnold Lamm → Steven Lamm. Public-facing physician; reachable via NYU Langone.

LIVING 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek Rapaport. International attorney; former IDF Military Judge; Hidden-Child Holocaust survivor (parents survived in Poland). Times of Israel blogger. Splits time NYC and Israel. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Isaac Chaim Griffel → Yehoshua Heshel "Henry" Griffel → Andrew. Public author page: https://www.timesofisrael.com/writers/andrew-griffel/
LIVING cousin via Isaac Chaim Griffel → Shmuel Shmelke Griffel → Boruch. Lives in Jerusalem. Named by Edward Gelles as a private-communication source on Griffel-Nadwórna research.

LIVING 3rd cousin of David Memek Rapaport. Lives in Israel. Edward Gelles's DNA-confirmed cousin via autosomal testing. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Chaya Griffel-Gruenfeld → Sima Gruenfeld-Blau → Clara Blau-Heyn → Pinhas.

LIVING son of Edward Gelles (the published genealogist of the Griffel-Nadwórna dynasty). Named in Gelles's PDFs as 'my son Haim, his spouse Shevi'. Has a son Avi Gelles. Likely UK/Israel. Contact via Balliol Archives (archives@balliol.ox.ac.uk).
LIVING grandson of Edward Gelles; named in Gelles's published Griffel-Nadwórna pedigree.

facts.value: archivist@balliol.ox.ac.uk (Balliol Modern Papers will forward)
An Ancient Lineage: European Roots of a Jewish Family (Vallentine Mitchell, 2006); 50+ research papers at Balliol archives

people.note_en: LIVING — age 98, London. 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek Rapaport. Born Vienna 24 November 1927. Published genealogist whose work at Balliol College Oxford documents the entire Griffel-Chajes-Wahl-Gelles dynasty of Galicia. Authored 'An Ancient Lineage: European Roots of a Jewish Family' (Vallentine Mitchell, 2006). His mother Regina Gelles née Griffel (1900-1954) was David Memek's first cousin. Senior living expert on our maternal line — knows everyone, holds Reb Eliezer's archive notes. Contact via Balliol Modern Papers archivist: archivist@balliol.ox.ac.uk

1st cousin of David Memek Rapaport. Famed Vaad ha-Hatzala rescuer in Istanbul during WWII, helping save Polish Jews. Subject of Joseph Friedenson's book 'Dateline: Istanbul: Dr Jacob Griffel's Lone Odyssey Through a Sea of Indifference' (ArtScroll, 1987). Son of Isaac Chaim Griffel + Judith Breit.

1st cousin once removed of David Memek Rapaport. Born Juliusz Gruenfeld in Lwów 1930. Holocaust survivor who lived under false papers in Aryan-side Warsaw and Lwów — the same world as our Lusia's survival story. Cornell Professor of Psychiatry. Author of the celebrated 1989 memoir 'The Lost Childhood: A Memoir'. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Chaya Griffel-Gruenfeld → Samuel Gruenfeld → Yehuda.

Documented in the Bolechów Yizkor 1957 (chapter 'Wood and Other Industries' by Abraham Weber, p.102) as a senior Jewish wood-industry specialist at the Bolechów timber factory. Likely a paternal uncle or older cousin of David Memek Rapaport given the surname, location, and forestry connection.

Documented in the Bolechów Yizkor 1957 (chapter 'Wood and Other Industries' by Abraham Weber, p.102) as owner of one of two large factories in Bolechów (a candle factory at the station). Likely a Weitzner uncle or cousin of Lusia/Leah.

Documented in the Bolechów Yizkor (bol086.html). A Bolechów youth who gave his free time to a youth-movement organization but died young of dysentery pre-war. Possibly a younger brother or cousin of Lusia.
1st cousin of David Memek. Son of David Mendel Griffel + Chawa Wahl. Born Nadwórna 1897, died 1951.

1st cousin of David Memek. Born Nadwórna 18 March 1900 (USHMM Passport Application). Married Dr David Isaac Gelles of Vienna. Reached England 13 Aug 1938 as Nazi refugee with husband and elder son Ludwig. Mother of the genealogist Edward Gelles. Blue eyes, blond hair, medium height (per her 1919 Polish passport application).
1st cousin of David Memek. Born Nadwórna 9 Oct 1904. Industrialist (Przemysłowiec) resident Stanisławów.

death.note_en: Murdered in 1941 — almost certainly the 6 October 1941 Bukowinka Forest mass shooting in Nadwórna.

people.note_en: Wife of David Mendel Griffel (Rebeka's eldest brother). Of the Wahl family — connecting to the Saul Wahl Katzenellenbogen rabbinical dynasty per Edward Gelles.
Husband of Machla Griffel-Wilner.
Husband of Zissel Griffel-Lamm. Father of Dr Arnold Lamm; grandfather of Dr Steven Lamm (NYC, living).
Son of Zissel + Zygmunt Lamm. Father of Dr Steven Lamm.
Wife of Isaac Chaim Griffel. Died 1938.
Son of Isaac Chaim Griffel + Judith Breit. Emigrated to New Jersey. Father of Andrew Griffel (NYC/Israel, living).
Husband of Chaya Griffel-Gruenfeld (1869-1953).

LIVING — NYC. 3rd cousin of David Memek Rapaport. Daughter of Andrew Griffel. Survivor of the Ras Burqa terror attack 5 October 1985 in Sinai — her mother Anita Spindel Griffel was murdered shielding 5-year-old Tali with her body. Andrew raised her in NYC. Now married, mother. Contributor to Tablet Magazine + Israel Story podcast.

facts.value: Tablet Magazine; Israel Story podcast; Instagram @ourfamilybikes

people.note_en: LIVING — only surviving child of Dr Jacob Griffel (1900-1962, the Vaad ha-Hatzala Istanbul rescuer). Jacob's first wife and children perished in the Warsaw blitzkrieg; Joseph from Jacob's second marriage to Miriam Rottenberg (1914-1998). 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek.
LIVING — NYC. New York Times staff reporter (long-form features, equestrian, real estate). Daughter of Yehuda Nir + Bonnie Maslin. 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek.

facts.value: https://www.nytimes.com/by/sarah-maslin-nir

people.note_en: LIVING — NYC. Political Director of Daily Kos. Son of Yehuda Nir + Bonnie Maslin. 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek. Contact: davidnir@dailykos.com / @DavidNir on Twitter.
LIVING — NYC. Private investor. Son of Yehuda Nir (first marriage). 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek.
LIVING — NYC. Fashion executive. Son of Yehuda Nir (first marriage). 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek.
LIVING — NYC. Widow of Yehuda Nir (d.2014). Psychiatrist/author (books on relationships). Likely warmest first-contact point for reaching the four Nir children.
LIVING — Rockville MD area. Widow of Eric Griffel (1930-2020, Edward Gelles's first cousin, USAID Pakistan Station Chief who signed the Blood Telegram). Contact via USAID Alumni Association.
Born London 27 Oct 1943. Daughter of Edward Griffel (uncle of David Memek) + Susan Manson. Status uncertain — would be 82. 2nd cousin of David Memek. Worth a targeted search.
